product_decision.selected_decision: Use the Home section `Tu Ruta de Claridad` as an orientation bridge toward `/simulador`, not as a full simulator, dashboard, offer page or data-capture flow.
product_decision.rationale: The user needs clarity and trust before advancing to the simulator; Home should prepare the decision path without collecting sensitive data or promising a result.
rejected_options: Turn the section into a full financial dashboard
Ask for sensitive data inside Home
Promise a guaranteed offer before validation
Convert the block into a broad educational module

included_scope: Explain the Ruta de Claridad as a short guided path
Preserve one primary CTA: Ver si tengo una oferta
State that alternatives or offers are referential and subject to validation
Prepare handoff for UX/UI, Copy, Legal/Data and QA
excluded_scope: Guaranteed offer or guaranteed financial result
Full payment simulator embedded in Home
Dashboard, account center or analytics view
Final visual layout or render specification

priority.reason: This block controls the transition from Home awareness to simulator action and reduces ambiguity before the user continues.

acceptance_criteria: The section has exactly one primary action leading to /simulador.
The section does not ask for DNI, income, debt amount, phone or other sensitive data.
Any mention of offer, alternative or payment option is explicitly referential and subject to validation.
UX/UI can design the section without inventing product intent or adding extra flows.
Copy can write microcopy without promising approval, savings or guaranteed benefit.
QA can reject the block if it becomes dashboard, checkout, long form, offer page or pressure-based conversion.
dependencies: UX/UI must transform the product decision into a section-level layout.
Copy must preserve non-pressure language and referential claims.
Legal/Data must verify sensitive-data and offer-claim boundaries.
QA must check CTA route, scope boundaries and prohibited claims.
risks: The section may drift into a simulator if too many details are added.
The CTA may imply a guaranteed offer if copy is not controlled.
Visual design may turn the path into a generic timeline or dashboard if UX intent is not protected.

next_step.instruction: Create a section-level UI spec for Tu Ruta de Claridad using this Product Direction Spec; do not invent simulator logic, legal claims or data capture.

open_assumptions: The phrase Ver si tengo una oferta remains the primary CTA unless governance changes it later.
The simulator route remains /simulador unless routing changes are approved separately.

success_metric_or_proxy.primary_proxy: User reaches /simulador from Home through a clear, non-pressure orientation section.
success_metric_or_proxy.quality_proxy: No sensitive-data request, no guaranteed claim, no dashboard drift, one primary CTA.

scope_boundaries: excluded sensitive data and guaranteed offer
future simulator logic remains outside Home
no_vague_language: clarity means observable route preparation with one CTA and explicit exclusions
